mission.title: rook: Could not access Ceph dashboard
mission.description: Could not access Ceph dashboard. Requested by 17+ users.

steps.title: Check current rook deployment
steps.description:
Verify your rook version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
helm list -n rook 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working rook installation.

steps.title: Review rook configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant rook configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook
```
**Is this a bug report or feature request?**
* Bug Report

**Deviation from expected behavior:**
using https://github.com/rook/rook/blob/master/cluster/examples/kubernetes/ceph/cluster.yaml which enabled dashboard default but I cannot access ceph

steps.title: Apply the fix for Could not access Ceph dashboard
steps.description:
Binding the dashboard and prometheus to all interfaces instead of the pod ip will ensure that they are listening properly. Binding to the pod ip was only a short term solution that was intended to be removed when deploying Nautilus, which is already Rook's min supported version.

This PR will
```yaml
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it $(kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod -l "app=rook-ceph-tools" -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- ceph config set mgr mgr/dashboard/server_addr 0.0.0.0

kubectl delete pod -n rook-ceph  $(kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod -l app=rook-ceph-mgr -o jsonpath="{.items[0].metadata.name}")

kubectl -n rook-ceph port-forward $(kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod -l app=rook-ceph-mgr -o jsonpath="{.items[0].metadata.name}") 8443:8443

curl -k https://localhost:8443
...
```

steps.title: Upgrade rook to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade rook:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade rook rook/rook --namespace rook
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook
helm list -n rook
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook
kubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Could not access Ceph dashboard" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Binding the dashboard and prometheus to all interfaces instead of the pod ip will ensure that they are listening properly. Binding to the pod ip was only a short term solution that was intended to be removed when deploying Nautilus, which is already Rook's min supported version.

This PR will ensure the new binding is applied for all upgrade clusters to the latest v1.5.
codeSnippets:
kubectl -n rook-ceph exec -it $(kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod -l "app=rook-ceph-tools" -o jsonpath='{.items[0].metadata.name}') -- ceph config set mgr mgr/dashboard/server_addr 0.0.0.0

kubectl delete pod -n rook-ceph  $(kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod -l app=rook-ceph-mgr -o jsonpath="{.items[0].metadata.name}")

kubectl -n rook-ceph port-forward $(kubectl -n rook-ceph get pod -l app=rook-ceph-mgr -o jsonpath="{.items[0].metadata.name}") 8443:8443

curl -k https://localhost:8443
...

$ kubectl proxy
Starting to serve on 127.0.0.1:8001
kubectl logs rook-ceph-mgr-a-[RANDOM]  -n rook-ceph | grep "Serving on"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with rook installed or the issue environment reproducible.
